cycle_goal: diagnose flat loss in nn_core tensor port

diagnosis.symptom: 500-step tensor-backed train run shows loss FLAT at 3.46574 (= ln(32) = ln(VOCAB)), zero delta across all steps
diagnosis.root_cause: BLAS matmul silently returns zeros — hxblas_sgemm call through FFI produces C[i]=0 for all elements. When matmul returns zeros, every layer's output collapses to zero (post-RMSNorm), all logits become identical, softmax yields uniform 1/V distribution, and loss = -log(1/V) = ln(32) = 3.46574. Gradients through uniform softmax are near-zero, so SGD updates are negligible and weights never move.
evidence: loss_first == loss_final == 3.46574 == ln(32) exactly — uniform random output, not partial learning
median_step_ms=0, p90=1000 — clock() returns integer seconds, indicating interpreter mode (not compiled). BLAS dlopen may succeed but FFI call semantics differ in interpreter vs compiled path

The BLAS spike (hexa_native_accelerate_spike_20260415) documents FFI_FLOAT_DOUBLE blocker: hexa_v2 maps Float->double in FFI typedefs, but cblas_sgemm expects float for alpha/beta. The C wrapper shim (hxblas_wrapper.c) correctly narrows double->float — but only when the compiled binary calls through the correct ABI. In interpreter mode, the FFI dispatch path may not invoke the shim correctly
Prior successful run (comment in bench_train_step_tensor.hexa line 784) shows loss 3.467->2.079 with 100 steps — that run was likely compiled via hexa build, not interpreted
The real_shape_bench (20260416) at larger shapes shows loss_decreased=1 for most configs but loss_decreased=0 for smallest (d=16) and some others — confirming the issue is shape/precision dependent
secondary_factors: PTR_OFFSET_IS_BYTE_NOT_ELEMENT: hexa_ptr_write_f32 uses byte offset. bench_train_step_tensor.hexa correctly multiplies by 4 (line 92: (i*d1+j)*4), so this is not the bug here — but any caller that forgets *4 would silently corrupt data
HEXA_ARRAY_COPY_O_N2: not the cause of flat loss but explains why non-BLAS fallback is 1000ms/step

most_likely_fix: Run bench_train_step_tensor via 'hexa build' + compiled binary with DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=training/build, NOT 'hexa run' interpreter. Alternatively, verify that interpreter-mode FFI dispatch calls hxblas_sgemm with correct register ABI on ARM64. The 100-step successful run (loss 3.47->2.08) was compiled; the 500-step flat run was interpreted.

next_perf_improvement.description: All per-element loops replaced with native C calls via hxblas_wrapper.c FFI. 14 extern declarations added, 12 hexa scalar loops replaced.
ops_offloaded: attn_fwd_t softmax+causal -> hxblas_attn_softmax_causal (+ QK^T via sgemm)
attn_bwd_t softmax_bwd -> hxblas_attn_softmax_bwd (+ dprobs/dV/dQ/dK via sgemm)
next_perf_improvement.expected_impact: 2-5x step latency reduction on top of BLAS matmul gains. Additionally, attn_fwd_t and attn_bwd_t inner loops replaced with BLAS matmul calls (QK^T, probs*V, dprobs, dV, dQ, dK) — removing O(S^2*D) interpreter overhead.

blocking_issues: FFI_FLOAT_DOUBLE in hexa_v2 codegen — prevents direct cblas_sgemm without wrapper shim (agent #18 scope)
Interpreter-mode FFI reliability — dlopen/dlsym path in interpreter may have different calling convention than compiled path
stage0 COW array semantics — needed for broad 1.3-2x gain on all 110 phi engines (medium-term, 2-4 days)
